content.full_text:
March 14, 1978


MR. Arthur A. Rodondii
City Clerk, City of South
San Francisco
P. O. Box 711
South San Francisco, California 94080

Dear Mr. Rodondii:

Thank you for your letter of March 2, 1978, asking what form
Mr. Brincat, a candidate for city council, must use to file his
post-election campaign statement. You mentioned that
Mr. Brincat and his committee have filed two pre-election state-
ments on Form 490, but because the treasurer of his committee
has resigned, there is some confusion as to which form must be
used for the third filing.

I am assuming that because a Form 490 was filed for the two pre-
election filings the committee is a recipient committee. As a
recipient committee a new treasurer must be appointed and an
amended Statement of Organization, Form 410, must be filed with
the Secretary of State's office reflecting the change. The
committee must not accept or expend any funds until it acquires
a new treasurer. As a recipient committee it does not automatically
terminate until it can meet certain criteria. Consequently, the
candidate should appoint a new treasurer and file the post-election
statement using the Form 490.

The committee may terminate only when all of the following conditions
are met:

1. The committee has ceased to receive contributions or make
expenditures and does not anticipate receiving contributions
or making expenditures in the future;

2. has eliminated or has declared that it has no intention or
ability to discharge all its debts, loans received and
other obligations;

Mr. Arthur A. Rodondi                    -2-                    March 14, 1978


3.  has no surplus funds; and

4.  has filed all required campaign statements disclosing all
reportable transactions.

When all of the above conditions have been met, the committee may
file a Form 415, Statement of Termination, with the Secretary of
State and all filing officers with whom previous campaign state-
ments have been filed.

Any surplus funds to be disposed of by the committee must comply
with Section VII of the Statement of Organization (Form 410) filed
with the Secretary of State. If it is determined that the
disposition will be different that stated, the Form 410 must be
amended to reflect the change.

In his letter to you of February 28, 1978, Mr. Brincat's former
treasurer, Mr. Moreno, stated that by his signed letter he will
not accept "liability or responsibility for any activity as the
result of this campaign." Perhaps you might want to inform
Mr. Moreno that during the period he served as treasurer to
Mr. Brincat he signed statements under penalty of perjury and has
accepted responsibility for the campaign during his tenure as
treasurer.

I hope the above answers your question. If I can be of further
assistance, do not hesitate to contact me.

                                        Sincerely,



                                        Mary Ann Kvasager
                                        Technical Assistance and
                                        Analysis Division

mak

OFFICE OF THE
CITY CLERK

March 2, 1978

Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Gentlemen:

Forwarded is a copy of a letter dated February 28, 1978 as received from
Doug Moreno, former Treasurer for Victor Brincat, Candidate for City Council
at the General Municipal Election to be held March 7, 1978, notifying this
office of his termination of services by the Candidate.

It would be appreciated if you would advise this office as to what is required
from the Candidate when he makes his third Campaign Statement with this office.

His previous two reportings were made on the Consolidated Campaign Statement
Form 490.

Very truly yours,

Arthur A. Rodondi, CMC
City Clerk

AAR:tg
Encl.

400 GRAND AVENUE - P. O. BOX 711 - 94080 - (415) 837-8000

Mr. Rodondi
City Clerk
City of South San Francisco
400 Grand Avenue
South San Francisco, Ca. 94080                    February 23, 1978

     Re: Campaign #780301
          Brincat for City Council
          South San Francisco

Dear Mr. Rodondi,

     Please be advised that as of 2/28/78 my services with
this campaign and Brincat were terminated by Brincat. Therefore,
effective with this notification I will no longer have any
involvement with either this campaign or Brincat. To this end
I will not accept any liability or responsibility for any
activity as the result of this campaign.


     cc: Secretary of State
          State Capitol
          925 L Street, Suite 605
          Sacramento, California     95814


                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Doug Moreno
                                        7423 Hillsdale Drive
                                        Pleasanton, Ca. 94566


[STAMP: RECEIVED MAR 1 1978 OFFICE OF CITY CLERK CITY OF SOUTH SAN FRANCISCO]

sections.question: What forms must a candidate for city council use for his campaign filings, and what is required when the treasurer changes before the third pre-election statement?
sections.conclusion: As a recipient committee, a new treasurer must be appointed and Form 410 (Statement of Organization) must be filed with the Secretary of State reflecting this change. The committee must not receive or expend any funds until it has appointed a new treasurer. A post-election statement using Form 490 must be filed. The committee may only terminate when all conditions are met: the candidate has declared no intention to seek office, there is no surplus funds, and all required statements have been filed.

sections.question_synthetic: The inquiry asks what reporting forms and procedures a candidate must follow when the campaign treasurer terminates services before the third pre-election filing for a city council election.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that a new treasurer must be appointed and Form 410 filed immediately; no funds may be received or expended without a treasurer in place. The committee must file the required post-election statement and can only terminate after meeting specific criteria including filing all required disclosures and having no remaining surplus.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: OCR quality affects accuracy; document contains multiple embedded letters. Clear QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections present but some OCR errors in text (e.g., '1918' appears to be '1978'). Confidence reduced due to OCR artifacts and text quality issues in scanned document.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What forms must a candidate for city council use for his campaign filings, and what is required when the treasurer changes before the third pre-election statement?

CONCLUSION: As a recipient committee, a new treasurer must be appointed and Form 410 (Statement of Organization) must be filed with the Secretary of State reflecting this change. The committee must not receive or expend any funds until it has appointed a new treasurer. A post-election statement using Form 490 must be filed. The committee may only terminate when all conditions are met: the candidate has declared no intention to seek office, there is no surplus funds, and all required statements have been filed.

embedding.summary: FPPC response letter to South San Francisco City Clerk regarding campaign reporting requirements for a city council candidate whose treasurer terminated services before the third filing deadline, clarifying treasurer succession procedures and committee termination criteria.
